hypotheses.label: Literal
hypotheses.deconstruct: Request: draft a message written from the Jeff company context that Justs will copy-paste to an unnamed recipient ('him'). Deliverable: a terminal-ready block of text describing 'the situation.' Output constraint: pasteable, no extra framing. Missing: who 'him' is, what aspect of 'the situation' to describe (onboarding, availability, role, something specific), and what tone is expected (formal/casual).
hypotheses.diagnose: Three gaps block clean execution: (1) 'from Jeff' is ambiguous — from the company Jeff, or impersonating a person named Jeff? (2) 'him' is unspecified — colleague, manager, external contact? (3) 'the situation' has no scope — new job start, current workload, a specific ask? Without these, the draft must assume maximally safe defaults.
hypotheses.develop: Task type: creative/professional writing. Role: ghostwriter speaking in Justs's voice from his Jeff Senior Data Analyst position. Optimization: assume the most probable read — Justs wants to inform someone in his network that he has just started at Jeff, is in onboarding mode, and has limited bandwidth. Keep it short, personal, no fluff. Structure: one paragraph, 3–5 sentences, terminal-pasteable.
hypotheses.deliver:
Draft message (copy-paste ready):

---
Hey — wanted to give you a quick heads-up. I just started my new role as Senior Data Analyst at Jeff this week, so I'm in full onboarding mode right now. Bandwidth is tight for the next few weeks while I get settled in. I'll reach out properly once I'm past the initial ramp. Talk soon.
---

hypotheses.label: Identity-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Justs is time-poor, results-only, and has zero patience for cognitive overhead. He asked for a message to copy-paste — he does not want to edit or workshop it. 'Describe the situation' means: capture enough context that the recipient understands why Justs is (or isn't) available, without requiring Justs to explain himself at length. The message is a functional handoff, not a relationship note.
hypotheses.diagnose: Justs's operating pattern means the message must be zero-friction on his end — finished on delivery. He will not revise. The risk is over-writing: a long message wastes his time and mismatches his voice, which is direct and terse. The 'situation' most relevant to his identity right now is the convergence of: new job started, daughter's concert today (May 14), emotional reserve near zero.
hypotheses.develop: Role: partner-as-ghostwriter, writing in Justs's compressed professional register. Technique: strip everything non-essential. One clear purpose per sentence. No pleasantries beyond the opener. Mirror his documented communication style: blunt, confident, forward-looking. The message should read as if Justs typed it himself in 45 seconds.
hypotheses.deliver:
Draft message (Justs's voice, identity-matched):

---
Just started at Jeff this week — Senior Data Analyst. Onboarding week, so I'm heads-down. Will be in touch when I've got my footing. Cheers.
---

hypotheses.label: Recall-shaped
hypotheses.deconstruct: Recall surfaces two dominant threads: (1) FUS046/FUS047 — Justs entered Jeff around May 11–13, 2026 with no internal relationships and emotional reserves already depleted; (2) FUS048 — Adrians Zeidmanis is the named contact Kha'an was directed to cultivate, with a ZWCAD/Claude Code angle. The highest-probability 'him' given current cortex evidence is Adrians or a professional contact Justs is updating on his new situation. The 'situation' in cortex terms is: new role, new org, zero established relationships inside Jeff, bandwidth constrained.
hypotheses.diagnose: The recall does not name the recipient explicitly, but Adrians is the only named third-party contact actively flagged for engagement. If the message is to Adrians, the framing shifts slightly — it's not just 'I started a new job' but also possibly 'here's my current bandwidth given the new role.' The prior outreach to Adrians was anchored in a specific ZWCAD bottleneck question, so the message might also re-engage that thread.
hypotheses.develop: Role: Kha'an drafting for Justs, with cortex knowledge of Adrians as the most likely recipient. Technique: keep the Jeff context as the main signal, briefly note bandwidth constraints, leave a hook for future contact. Do not reference Brain or Kha'an — this is a message Justs sends as himself. Educational frame: few-shot not needed; single clean draft with context-aware framing.
hypotheses.deliver:
Draft message (Adrians-aware framing):

---
Hey Adrians — quick update: I started a new role this week, Senior Data Analyst at Jeff. Onboarding week is pretty compressed so I'm low-bandwidth until I get settled. Still want to circle back on the ZWCAD question — will ping you properly in a week or two once things stabilize.
---

hypotheses.label: Contrarian
hypotheses.deconstruct: What if 'Jeff' is not the company but a person named Jeff — specifically someone at the Jeff organization Justs needs to reach out to? In that case, Justs is drafting an outreach TO Jeff (a colleague, manager, or internal contact at his new employer), introducing himself or describing his situation as a new hire. 'Post in the terminal' could mean a Slack/Teams terminal or internal chat system used at Jeff.
hypotheses.diagnose: If Jeff is a person, the message purpose flips: it becomes an introductory or status message from Justs to an internal Jeff contact, not an external update. The tone would be slightly more formal or professional-peer. 'Describe the situation' would mean: explain who Justs is, that he just joined, and what he needs (intro, meeting, access, a question answered). This is a plausible read because new hires routinely need to reach out to specific people in their first week.
hypotheses.develop: Adversarial read forces a draft that works if Jeff is a person inside the organization. Role: Justs as new hire reaching out to a colleague or manager named Jeff. Technique: professional intro register, brief context, clear ask or next step. Avoid the external-contact assumptions of H1–H3. Make it feel like a first-week internal Slack message.
hypotheses.deliver:
Draft message (if Jeff is a person, internal outreach):

---
Hi Jeff — I'm Justs, just joined this week as Senior Data Analyst. Wanted to introduce myself and get on your radar. Happy to jump on a quick call when you have 15 minutes — would be useful to understand how our work overlaps. Let me know what works.
---

hypotheses.label: Minimal-action
hypotheses.deconstruct: The smallest satisfying action: produce one terminal-pasteable message block that covers the core situation — Justs started at Jeff, is in onboarding mode, and has limited availability. No options, no variants, no back-and-forth. One output, done.
hypotheses.diagnose: Over-delivering (multiple variants, questions about the recipient, tone options) adds cognitive load Justs has explicitly rejected. The minimal action is to make a confident default assumption and produce one clean draft. If wrong, he will say so in one word.
hypotheses.develop: Technique: Occam's razor. Default assumptions: recipient is a professional contact outside Jeff, tone is casual-professional, length is 3–4 sentences, no sign-off beyond 'cheers' or 'talk soon.' Do not ask for clarification. Ship the draft.
hypotheses.deliver:
Draft message (minimal, terminal-ready):

---
Started a new role this week — Senior Data Analyst at Jeff. In onboarding mode so response times will be slow for a bit. Will be back in touch properly once I'm through the first few weeks.
---
